title: Visually assess standard deviation
description: Practice estimating standard deviation from data displays and comparing the standard deviations between sets of data.

assessment_items.question_content:
Each dot plot below represents a different set of data.

**Order the data sets from largest standard deviation (top) to smallest standard deviation (bottom).**

[[☃ sorter 1]]
hints: Standard deviation gives an overall measure of how spread out individual data points are from their mean. Data sets with more spread from the mean have larger standard deviations, and data sets with less spread from the mean have a smaller standard deviations.

Each dot plot shows data that appear to be symmetric, so the mean will be in the middle. All three data sets have the same range and the same mean. 

Some of the points are the same in all the graphs. Let's compare the spread of the data points that differ. Which data set has the most spread from the mean, and which has the least?

![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at 50 and 100. 6 dots are plotted in 2 groups of 2 each above 50 and 100, and 2 groups of 1 each, equidistant from 50 and 100, respectively, with a larger gap near the center of the axis. A dashed vertical line rises through the center of the axis. The top dot above both 50 and 100 is purple.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/fb8d3ac07eaeeabd6bdb47366fc89f94ddd6282e)

![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at 50 and 100. 6 dots are plotted individually with 1 dot above 50 and 1 above 100. The other dots are plotted with 2 dots evenly spaced rightward of 50 and 2 dots evenly spaced leftward of 100 with a larger gap near the center of the axis. The result appears to be a row of 3 dots starting at 50 followed by a gap, then a row of 3 dots ending at 100. A dashed vertical line rises through the center of the axis. The middle dot on either side of center is purple.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e6d8982a2116370d761274b78a6b4656ccf13860)

![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at 50 and 100. 6 dots are plotted in 2 groups of 1 each above 50 and 100, and 2 groups of 2 each, equidistant from 50 and 100, respectively, with a larger gap near the center of the axis. A dashed vertical line rises through the center of the axis. The top dot in each of the middle groups is purple.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4b9201896415757b18255f507f056b65283d9a4e)

###The answer

These are the data sets from largest standard deviation (top) to smallest standard deviation (bottom):
  
![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at 50 and 100. 6 dots are plotted in 2 groups of 2 each above 50 and 100, and 2 groups of 1 each, equidistant from 50 and 100, respectively, with a larger gap near the center of the axis.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b58bef623c79117425c5b672cdd00999e87e8528)

![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at 50 and 100. 6 dots are plotted individually with 1 dot above 50 and 1 above 100. The other dots are plotted with 2 dots evenly spaced rightward of 50 and 2 dots evenly spaced leftward of 100 with a larger gap near the center of the axis. The result appears to be a row of 3 dots starting at 50 followed by a gap, then a row of 3 dots ending at 100.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ed0121a46c0b44056ae4f98d2d476871c734eedd)

![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at 50 and 100. 6 dots are plotted in 2 groups of 1 each above 50 and 100, and 2 groups of 2 each, equidistant from 50 and 100, respectively, with a larger gap near the center of the axis.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cfc5ffe544c03124becfa953e69a6ce4a2d55eeb)

correct: ![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at 50 and 100. 6 dots are plotted in 2 groups of 2 each above 50 and 100, and 2 groups of 1 each, equidistant from 50 and 100, respectively, with a larger gap near the center of the axis.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b58bef623c79117425c5b672cdd00999e87e8528)

![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at 50 and 100. 6 dots are plotted individually with 1 dot above 50 and 1 above 100. The other dots are plotted with 2 dots evenly spaced rightward of 50 and 2 dots evenly spaced leftward of 100 with a larger gap near the center of the axis. The result appears to be a row of 3 dots starting at 50 followed by a gap, then a row of 3 dots ending at 100.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ed0121a46c0b44056ae4f98d2d476871c734eedd)

![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at 50 and 100. 6 dots are plotted in 2 groups of 1 each above 50 and 100, and 2 groups of 2 each, equidistant from 50 and 100, respectively, with a larger gap near the center of the axis.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cfc5ffe544c03124becfa953e69a6ce4a2d55eeb)

assessment_items.question_content:
Each dot plot below represents a different set of data.

**Order the data sets from largest standard deviation (top) to smallest standard deviation (bottom).**

[[☃ sorter 1]]
hints: Standard deviation gives an overall measure of how spread out individual data points are from their mean. Data sets with more spread from the mean have larger standard deviations, and data sets with less spread from the mean have a smaller standard deviations.

Each dot plot shows data that appear to be symmetric, so the mean will be in the middle. All three data sets have the same range and the same mean. 

Some of the points are the same in all the graphs. Let's compare the spread of the data points that differ. Which data set has the most spread from the mean, and which has the least?

![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at 0 and 20. 7 dots are plotted in 2 groups of 3 each above 0 and 20 with 1 dot plotted in the center. A dashed vertical line rises through the center of the axis and the dot above it. The top 2 points above both 0 and 20 are purple.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a904d69ac2843ec9e103eaf9a1e559c4fc8503cc)

![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at 0 and 20. 7 dots are plotted in 2 groups of 2 each above 0 and 20 with 3 single dots plotted at equally spaced intervals in between. A dashed vertical line rises through the center of the axis and the dot above it. The top points above 0 and 20, and the first and third point in between 0 and 20 are purple.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/938805dfe26091260633f6b51c563ce86aada472)

![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at 0 and 20. 7 dots are plotted in 5 equally spaced groups of 1, 1, 3, 1, and 1, respectively. A dashed vertical line rises through the center of the axis and the dots above it. The 2 top dots above the center and each of the dots right or left of center are purple.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/347f6a25f1704014ed6983719a900e1909e81c36)

###The answer

These are the data sets from largest standard deviation (top) to smallest standard deviation (bottom):
  
![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at 0 and 20. 7 dots are plotted in 2 groups of 3 each above 0 and 20 with 1 dot plotted in the center.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c8671d9c4674c08914796118cf86913c8761ce8b)

![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at 0 and 20. 7 dots are plotted in 2 groups of 2 each above 0 and 20 with 3 single dots plotted at equally spaced intervals in between.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/da708ba588c630ad9f9646bc1884200880f96bf8)

![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at 0 and 20. 7 dots are plotted in 5 equally spaced groups of 1, 1, 3, 1, and 1, respectively.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/16cec9cda250c7ce7d938352352d29106abe2041)

correct: ![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at 0 and 20. 7 dots are plotted in 2 groups of 3 each above 0 and 20 with 1 dot plotted in the center.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c8671d9c4674c08914796118cf86913c8761ce8b)

![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at 0 and 20. 7 dots are plotted in 2 groups of 2 each above 0 and 20 with 3 single dots plotted at equally spaced intervals in between.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/da708ba588c630ad9f9646bc1884200880f96bf8)

![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at 0 and 20. 7 dots are plotted in 5 equally spaced groups of 1, 1, 3, 1, and 1, respectively.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/16cec9cda250c7ce7d938352352d29106abe2041)

assessment_items.question_content:
Each dot plot below represents a different set of data.

**Order the data sets from largest standard deviation (top) to smallest standard deviation (bottom).**

[[☃ sorter 1]]
hints: Standard deviation gives an overall measure of how spread out individual data points are from their mean. Data sets with more spread from the mean have larger standard deviations, and data sets with less spread from the mean have a smaller standard deviations.

Each dot plot shows data that appear to be symmetric, so the mean will be in the middle. All three data sets have the same range and the same mean. 

Some of the points are the same in all the graphs. Let's compare the spread of the data points that differ. Which data set has the most spread from the mean, and which has the least?

![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at negative 10 and 10. 7 dots are plotted at evenly spaced intervals between negative 10 and 10. The dots above negative 10, 10 and midway between are blue. A dashed vertical line rises through the axis and the dot at the center. The 2 dots between negative 10 and the middle and the 2 dots between the middle and 10 are purple.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5b7c4d9e6ce48a4e185c845896bd5ec6d5235dcd)

![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at negative 10 and 10. Both negative 10 and 10 have a single dot plotted above them. 5 additional dots are plotted at 3 evenly spaced intervals between negative 10 and 10, grouped 1, 3, and 1, respectively. The dots above negative 10 and 10, and the bottom dot midway between are blue. A dashed vertical line rises through the axis and the 3 dots at the center. The dot between negative 10 and the middle, the 2 upper dots above the middle, and the dot between the middle and 10 are purple.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3476ac9ef20b7ea6c6c883e0d85c294d2c67eea2)

![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at negative 10 and 10. Both negative 10 and 10 have a single dot plotted above them. 5 additional dots are plotted in 1 group midway between negative 10 and 10. The dots above negative 10, 10 and midway between are blue. A dashed vertical line rises through the axis and the dot at the center. The 4 upper dots above the middle are purple.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a22bad916a66283eee2ded4ebba7472e8bd35678)

###The answer

These are the data sets from largest standard deviation (top) to smallest standard deviation (bottom):
  
![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at negative 10 and 10. 7 dots are plotted, at evenly spaced intervals between negative 10 and 10.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8a044eb966cad4309c37928c6ddde548ebcb1095)

![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at negative 10 and 10. Both negative 10 and 10 have a single dot plotted above them. 5 additional dots are plotted at 3 evenly spaced intervals between negative 10 and 10, grouped 1, 3, and 1, respectively.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5c071975c6b157d63975f0eb53959ac5713d085e)

![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at negative 10 and 10. Both negative 10 and 10 have a single dot plotted above them. 5 additional dots are plotted in 1 group midway between negative 10 and 10.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/579d23f7123e9c1cb879b4a80c97bc541482d0bd)

correct: ![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at negative 10 and 10. 7 dots are plotted at evenly spaced intervals between negative 10 and 10.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8a044eb966cad4309c37928c6ddde548ebcb1095)

![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at negative 10 and 10. Both negative 10 and 10 have a single dot plotted above them. 5 additional dots are plotted at 3 evenly spaced intervals between negative 10 and 10, grouped 1, 3, and 1, respectively.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5c071975c6b157d63975f0eb53959ac5713d085e)

![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at negative 10 and 10. Both negative 10 and 10 have a single dot plotted above them. 5 additional dots are plotted in 1 group midway between negative 10 and 10.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/579d23f7123e9c1cb879b4a80c97bc541482d0bd)

assessment_items.question_content:
Each dot plot below represents a different set of data.

**Order the data sets from largest standard deviation (top) to smallest standard deviation (bottom).**

[[☃ sorter 1]]
hints: Standard deviation gives an overall measure of how spread out individual data points are from their mean. Data sets with more spread from the mean have larger standard deviations, and data sets with less spread from the mean have a smaller standard deviations.

Each dot plot shows data that appear to be symmetric, so the mean will be in the middle. All three data sets have the same range and the same mean. 

Some of the points are the same in all the graphs. Let's compare the spread of the data points that differ. Which data set has the most spread from the mean, and which has the least?

This graph has $4$ data points relatively far away from the mean:

![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at 1 and 6. Both 1 and 6 have 3 dots plotted above them. An additional 6 dots are plotted in 4 evenly spaced groups of 2, 1, 1, and 2, respectively. A dashed vertical line rises through the axis mid way between 1 and 6. The upper 2 dots above both 1 and 6 are purple.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/326e3ef4cd2f869d989c6cfea7f688ac748ed2b7)

This graph has $2$ data points that are relatively far and $2$ that are relatively close:

![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at 1 and 6. 12 dots are plotted in 4 evenly spaced groups of 2 between 1 and 6. A dashed vertical line rises through the axis mid way between 1 and 6. The top dots above 1 and 6 as well as the top dots in each of the 2 middle groups, are purple.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1de1c14888445b05e8e39d67c12b4b5e61cce945)

This graph has $4$ data points relatively close to the mean:

![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at 1 and 6. Both 1 and 6 have 1 dot plotted above them. An additional 10 dots are plotted in 4 evenly spaced groups of 2, 3, 3, and 2, respectively. A dashed vertical line rises through the axis mid way between 1 and 6. The top 2 dots in each of the 2 middle groups are purple.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/dc19f769a01e0d035fe91f4fa2085eda527bf9d3)

###The answer

These are the data sets from largest standard deviation (top) to smallest standard deviation (bottom):
  
![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at 1 and 6. Both 1 and 6 have 3 dots plotted above them. An additional 6 dots are plotted in 4 evenly spaced groups of 2, 1, 1, and 2, respectively.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ccb53dc128f65ed40bc5c7f1ab21e6ab78728f70)

![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at 1 and 6. 12 dots are plotted in 6 evenly spaced groups of 2 between 1 and 6.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1f2d7252ef26c3f3eed4e4a793aa85d10f58ebc8)

![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at 1 and 6. Both 1 and 6 have 1 dot plotted above them. An additional 10 dots are plotted in 4 evenly spaced groups of 2, 3, 3, and 2, respectively.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7ad0cf4daf2c9bcb43eda6c372582136cbfb68f3)

correct: ![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at 1 and 6. Both 1 and 6 have 3 dots plotted above them. An additional 6 dots are plotted in 4 evenly spaced groups of 2, 1, 1, and 2, respectively.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ccb53dc128f65ed40bc5c7f1ab21e6ab78728f70)

![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at 1 and 6. 12 dots are plotted in 6 evenly spaced groups of 2 between 1 and 6.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1f2d7252ef26c3f3eed4e4a793aa85d10f58ebc8)

![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at 1 and 6. Both 1 and 6 have 1 dot plotted above them. An additional 10 dots are plotted in 4 evenly spaced groups of 2, 3, 3, and 2, respectively.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7ad0cf4daf2c9bcb43eda6c372582136cbfb68f3)

assessment_items.question_content:
Each dot plot below represents a different set of data.

**Order the data sets from largest standard deviation (top) to smallest standard deviation (bottom).**

[[☃ sorter 1]]
hints: Standard deviation gives an overall measure of how spread out individual data points are from their mean. Data sets with more spread from the mean have larger standard deviations, and data sets with less spread from the mean have a smaller standard deviations.

Each dot plot shows data that appear to be symmetric, so the mean will be in the middle. All three data sets have the same mean. 

Some of the points are the same between two of the graphs. Let's compare the spread of the data points that differ. Which data set has the most spread from the mean, and which has the least?

![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at 0 and 100. 6 dots are plotted 3 each above 0 and 100. A dashed vertical line rises through the center of the axis. The top dot above 0 and the top dot above 100 are purple.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/874557de71cee2147ae070f825f8ac59a2840bbf)

![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at 0 and 100. 6 dots are plotted at 3 evenly spaced groups of 2 between 0 and 100. A dashed vertical line rises through the center of the axis and the 2 dots above it. The 2 dots above the center are purple.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/39abf2957403acbd24393e761deb33c03c4bb811)

This graph shows data with the *least* overall spread of the three, since all of the data points are relatively close to the mean:

![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at 0 and 100. 6 dots are plotted 3 each in 2 groups evenly spaced between 0 and 100, with none over 0 or 100. A dashed vertical line rises through the center of the axis leaving 1 group on either side.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/bd6d58c755d867cf590c6ea0569a2f2225323eac)

###The answer

These are the data sets from largest standard deviation (top) to smallest standard deviation (bottom):
  
![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at 0 and 100. 6 dots are plotted 3 each above 0 and 100.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6c817a32e31567b9b5e7c34cff564da30541f29c)

![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at 0 and 100. 6 dots are plotted at 3 evenly spaced groups of 2 between 0 and 100.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/03ad0b66b0cc7121b876429f5162111dcbe19869)

![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at 0 and 100. 6 dots are plotted 3 each in 2 groups evenly spaced between 0 and 100, with none over 0 or 100.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9854e521e13df661dd7b35b23071730041ac0815)

correct: ![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at 0 and 100. 6 dots are plotted 3 each above 0 and 100.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6c817a32e31567b9b5e7c34cff564da30541f29c)

![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at 0 and 100. 6 dots are plotted at 3 evenly spaced groups of 2 between 0 and 100.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/03ad0b66b0cc7121b876429f5162111dcbe19869)

![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at 0 and 100. 6 dots are plotted 3 each in 2 groups evenly spaced between 0 and 100, with none over 0 or 100.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9854e521e13df661dd7b35b23071730041ac0815)

assessment_items.question_content:
Each dot plot below represents a different set of data.

**Order the data sets from largest standard deviation (top) to smallest standard deviation (bottom).**

[[☃ sorter 1]]
hints: Standard deviation gives an overall measure of how spread out individual data points are from their mean. Data sets with more spread from the mean have larger standard deviations, and data sets with less spread from the mean have a smaller standard deviations.

Each dot plot shows data that appear to be symmetric, so the mean will be in the middle. All three data sets have the same mean. 

Some of the points are the same between two of the graphs. Let's compare the spread of the data points that differ. Which data set has the most spread from the mean, and which has the least?

![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at 0 and 100. 8 dots are plotted in 2 groups of 2 each above 0 and 100, respectively, and 2 groups of 2 each, equidistant from 0 and 100, respectively, with a larger gap near the center of the axis. A dashed vertical line rises through the center of the axis. Both dots in the second and third groups are purple.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5a3f247877bb3d484c650874cf07e6568a2ce624)

![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at 0 and 100. 8 dots are plotted 2 groups of 2 above 0 and 100, respectively, and 2 groups of 2, just left and right of the center of the axis. A dashed vertical line rises through the center of the axis. Both dots in the second and third groups are purple.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/41f88063be4861a43daa7deec8b66c811dfc4fab)

This graph shows data with the *least* overall spread of the three, since all of the data points are relatively closer to the mean:

![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at 0 and 100. 8 dots are plotted 4 groups of 2 equally spaced around the center of the axis with no dots above 0 or 100. A dashed vertical line rises through the center of the axis between the second and third groups.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/964e8153d9644ae70847aad37cfaf322f4ec50e9)

###The answer

These are the data sets from largest standard deviation (top) to smallest standard deviation (bottom):
  
![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at 0 and 100. 8 dots are plotted in 2 groups of 2 each above 0 and 100, respectively, and 2 groups of 2 each, equidistant from 0 and 100, respectively, with a larger gap near the center of the axis.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5e79cf9c27030fd593f89b57c6671b9b2bd51b88)

![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at 0 and 100. 8 dots are plotted 2 groups of 2 above 0 and 100, respectively, and 2 groups of 2, just left and right of the center of the axis.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/895349a6546f7a5f7abde79046634a349a23c5b7)

![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at 0 and 100. 8 dots are plotted 4 groups of 2 equally spaced around the center of the axis with no dots above 0 or 100.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/dff9ee2b88b1f34798ac293577dd0e18e1f03cf0)

correct: ![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at 0 and 100. 8 dots are plotted in 2 groups of 2 each above 0 and 100, respectively, and 2 groups of 2 each, equidistant from 0 and 100, respectively, with a larger gap near the center of the axis.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5e79cf9c27030fd593f89b57c6671b9b2bd51b88)

![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at 0 and 100. 8 dots are plotted 2 groups of 2 above 0 and 100, respectively, and 2 groups of 2, just left and right of the center of the axis.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/895349a6546f7a5f7abde79046634a349a23c5b7)

![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at 0 and 100. 8 dots are plotted 4 groups of 2 equally spaced around the center of the axis with no dots above 0 or 100.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/dff9ee2b88b1f34798ac293577dd0e18e1f03cf0)

**Order the data sets from largest standard deviation (top) to smallest standard deviation (bottom).**

hints: Standard deviation gives an overall measure of how spread out individual data points are from their mean. Data sets with more spread from the mean have larger standard deviations, and data sets with less spread from the mean have a smaller standard deviations.

assessment_items.question_content:
Each dot plot below represents a different set of data.

**Order the data sets from largest standard deviation (top) to smallest standard deviation (bottom).**

[[☃ sorter 1]]
hints: Standard deviation gives an overall measure of how spread out individual data points are from their mean. Data sets with more spread from the mean have larger standard deviations, and data sets with less spread from the mean have a smaller standard deviations.

Each dot plot shows data that appear to be symmetric, so the mean will be in the middle. All three data sets have the same mean. 

This graph shows data with the *most* overall spread of the three, since the data points are relatively farther from the mean:

![A dot plot has an unlabeled horizontal axis marked at 100 and 200. 10 dots are plotted in 2 groups of 4 above 100 and 200, respectively, and 2 groups of 1, just left and right of the center of the axis. A dashed vertical line rises through the center of the axis. All dots above 100 and 200 are purple.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5c52f44b90c33177cd9f66e4c0fc5b47ed7b341b)
